title: 7 Best Herbal Teas to Help Reduce High Cholesterol

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for high cholesterol because it helps to lower bad cholesterol levels in the blood by preventing the absorption of dietary cholesterol and reducing inflammation in the blood vessels.

Bilobalide is good for high cholesterol because it helps to lower triglycerides and improve blood lipid profiles by inhibiting the activity of the enzyme HMG-CoA reductase, which is involved in the production of cholesterol in the liver.

Kaempferol is good for high cholesterol because it helps lower cholesterol levels by blocking the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut and increasing the expression of genes that remove excess cholesterol from the body.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba decoctions may help reduce high cholesterol. The leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which are compounds that have been shown to improve blood circulation and lower cholesterol levels. When taken as a decoction, these compounds can help remove excess cholesterol from the bloodstream and reduce the risk of plaque buildup in the arteries. This can lead to improved heart health and lower the risk of heart disease. Ginkgo biloba decoctions may also help relax blood vessels, making it easier for blood to flow through them, which can further help lower cholesterol levels. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba decoctions can help protect against cell damage and inflammation, which can contribute to high cholesterol. By reducing inflammation and improving blood circulation, Ginkgo biloba decoctions may help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol and increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia miltiorrhiza herbal decoction that can help with high cholesterol, start by gathering dried Salvia miltiorrhiza roots. You can purchase these roots from a health food store or online. Rinse the roots with clean water and chop them into smaller pieces. Combine 10-15 grams of chopped roots with 1 liter of boiling water in a saucepan. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for about 30 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the decoction will be. Once it has cooled, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the roots. You can store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use it, drink 1/2 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day before meals. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before making any changes to your diet or supplement routine, especially if you're taking medications or have any underlying health conditions.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for high cholesterol because it helps lower 'bad' LDL cholesterol levels and increase 'good' HDL cholesterol levels, thus improving overall heart health by reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases.

Allicin is good for high cholesterol because it helps to lower bad cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and improving the function of the bile, which helps to remove excess cholesterol from the body.

Polyphenols are good for high cholesterol because they help lower cholesterol levels by reducing the amount of bad cholesterol in the blood and also improve the functioning of the liver to remove excess cholesterol.

Saponins are good for high cholesterol because they help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels by preventing the formation of new cholesterol and improving the body's ability to remove excess cholesterol.
Flavonoids are good for high cholesterol because they help lower LDL cholesterol and increase HDL cholesterol by reducing inflammation in blood vessels and improving blood lipid profiles.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum, also known as garlic, has been used for centuries to lower cholesterol levels. The active compounds in garlic, such as allicin, work by reducing the formation of cholesterol in the liver. This is because allicin blocks an enzyme that helps create cholesterol. When this enzyme is blocked, less cholesterol is produced in the liver, and more is removed from the bloodstream. Garlic also helps improve blood circulation, which can lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease. Additionally, garlic has been shown to increase the levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) or "good" cholesterol in the blood, which helps remove excess cholesterol from the bloodstream.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia miltiorrhiza herbal decoctions that can help with high cholesterol, start by gathering dried Salvia miltiorrhiza roots, which are commonly known as Danshen. You will need 6-12 grams of the dried roots for every 2 cups of water. In a pot, combine the dried roots and the water, then bring the mixture to a boil over high heat. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and let the decoction cook for 30-40 minutes, allowing the roots to release their active compounds into the water. After the decoction has cooled, you can strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids. You can store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days, or freeze it for later use. When ready to drink, take 1/2 to 1 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day, ideally after meals, to help lower your cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol helps lower high cholesterol by reducing the amount of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) or bad cholesterol in the blood, which can block arteries and cause heart problems.
Polyphenols are good for high cholesterol because they help to reduce the amount of bad cholesterol in the blood and also increase the production of good cholesterol.
Shogaol is good for high cholesterol because it helps lower bad cholesterol levels by inhibiting the production of a protein that contributes to the formation of fatty deposits in the arteries.

Zingiberene is good for high cholesterol because it helps lower bad cholesterol levels by reducing the production of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) and increasing the removal of it from the bloodstream, which can help prevent plaque buildup in arteries.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help lower cholesterol levels. When made into a decoction, the active compounds in ginger work to reduce inflammation in the body, which is a major contributor to high cholesterol. This inflammation can cause the formation of plaque in the arteries, leading to high cholesterol. Ginger decoctions have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce this inflammation, thereby reducing the risk of plaque formation. Additionally, ginger decoctions have been shown to improve blood lipid profiles by increasing the levels of high-density lipoprotein (HDL) or "good" cholesterol and decreasing the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) or "bad" cholesterol. This can help to reduce the risk of heart disease and other cardiovascular problems associated with high cholesterol.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia miltiorrhiza herbal decoction to help with high cholesterol, you will need some dried Salvia miltiorrhiza roots, a large pot, and water. First, rinse the dried Salvia miltiorrhiza roots with clean water, then put them in the large pot. Next, add about 10-15 cups of water to the pot, making sure the roots are completely covered. Now, bring the water to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer. Let the roots steep in the water for about 1-2 hours, or until the liquid has reduced by half. You can check the liquid's consistency by tasting it - if it tastes strong, it's ready. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve to remove the roots, and discard the roots. Let the decoction cool down before drinking it. It's recommended to drink 1-2 cups of the Salvia miltiorrhiza decoction per day, preferably after meals, to help lower your cholesterol levels. However, please consult a healthcare professional before using this herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Saponins are good for high cholesterol because they help lower blood fat levels by inhibiting the production of cholesterol in the liver and improving the body's ability to remove excess cholesterol.
Polyphenols are good for high cholesterol because they help lower cholesterol levels by reducing the amount of bad cholesterol in the blood and preventing it from building up on artery walls.

Ginsenosides are good for high cholesterol because they help lower bad cholesterol and triglyceride levels in the blood by increasing the breakdown and removal of fat and improving blood vessel function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng decoctions are a type of traditional medicine that may help lower high cholesterol levels. Ginseng contains compounds called ginsenosides, which have been shown to have positive effects on the body's cholesterol levels. When taken as a decoction, these compounds are thought to help increase the levels of HDL (good) cholesterol in the blood. This can help counteract the effects of LDL (bad) cholesterol and reduce the risk of heart disease. Additionally, ginseng decoctions may also help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels. Some studies suggest that ginseng decoctions may also help to improve blood flow and lower blood pressure, further reducing the risk of heart disease. However, more research is needed to fully understand the effects of Panax ginseng decoctions on high cholesterol levels.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia miltiorrhiza herbal decoction that helps with high cholesterol, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Salvia miltiorrhiza roots, which can be found at a local health food store or online. Rinse the roots with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, combine 10-15 grams of the dried roots with 2 cups of water in a saucepan. Bring the water to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 20-30 minutes. The longer it cooks, the stronger the decoction will be. After the decoction has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids. You can store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use it, drink 1/2 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day. It's recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using Salvia miltiorrhiza, especially if you have any underlying health conditions or are taking medications.

Tannins have been found to be good for high cholesterol because they help to reduce the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) or "bad" cholesterol in the blood by acting as antioxidants and preventing the formation of plaque in arteries, which in turn lowers the risk of heart disease and other cardiovascular conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata decoctions have been used in traditional medicine to help lower high cholesterol levels. The plant contains compounds that can reduce the levels of bad cholesterol (LDL) in the blood. LDL is a type of fat that can build up in the arteries and cause heart problems. Cassia auriculata decoctions may also help increase the levels of good cholesterol (HDL) in the blood. HDL helps remove bad cholesterol from the arteries, making them healthier. The decoctions may also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals can contribute to the formation of plaque in the arteries, which can lead to heart disease. By reducing the levels of bad cholesterol and protecting the body from free radicals, Cassia auriculata decoctions may help lower high cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of heart disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia miltiorrhiza herbal decoction for high cholesterol, start by gathering 6-8 grams of dried Salvia miltiorrhiza root, which is also known as Danshen. Rinse the root with clean water to remove any impurities. Next, place the root in a saucepan and add 2 cups of water. Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to a simmer for 30 minutes to allow the root's active compounds to infuse into the water. After the decoction is ready, let it cool down before straining it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl. Discard the root and store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use, drink 1/2 cup of the decoction 2-3 times a day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or take medications.

Polyphenols are good for high cholesterol because they help lower bad cholesterol and triglycerides by preventing the formation of fatty deposits in the blood vessels.

Cinnamaldehyde is good for high cholesterol because it helps to reduce the level of bad cholesterol in the blood by inhibiting the production of a protein that increases cholesterol levels, which in turn can lower the risk of heart disease.
Coumarins are good for high cholesterol because they help lower cholesterol levels by reducing the amount of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol, also known as bad cholesterol, in the blood.

Tannins are good for high cholesterol because they help to reduce the production of bad cholesterol in the body by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme called HMG-CoA reductase, which is involved in the formation of cholesterol.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum decoction, also known as Ceylon cinnamon, is a natural remedy that may help lower high cholesterol levels. This spice contains a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed as a decoction, the cinnamaldehyde in Ceylon cinnamon may help reduce the absorption of dietary cholesterol in the gut. Additionally, it may also improve the functioning of the liver, which plays a crucial role in removing excess cholesterol from the body. Studies have also suggested that Ceylon cinnamon may help increase the levels of good cholesterol (HDL) in the blood, while also reducing the levels of bad cholesterol (LDL). The decoction may also help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which is a major risk factor for heart disease. By incorporating Ceylon cinnamon into the diet, individuals with high cholesterol may be able to improve their cholesterol profiles and reduce their risk of heart disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia miltiorrhiza herbal decoction that helps with high cholesterol, start by gathering dried Salvia miltiorrhiza roots. Measure out about 6-8 grams of the dried roots and place them in a heat-resistant container. Next, boil 2 cups of water in a separate pot and then carefully pour the hot water over the dried roots. Allow the mixture to steep for 30-40 minutes, or until the water has cooled to room temperature. After the steeping process, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids. You can now store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days. To use, drink 1-2 cups of the decoction per day, preferably after meals, as the saponins in Salvia miltiorrhiza may help lower cholesterol levels and improve overall heart health.

Rosmarinic acid is good for high cholesterol because it helps lower bad cholesterol levels by reducing the absorption of dietary fats and also by preventing the formation of new cholesterol in the liver.
Tanshinone I is good for high cholesterol because it helps remove excess cholesterol from the blood by increasing the liver's ability to break down LDL (bad) cholesterol.

Polyphenols are good for high cholesterol because they help lower the bad cholesterol (LDL) levels in the blood by preventing the absorption of cholesterol in the digestive system, thereby reducing the risk of heart disease and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Flavonoids are good for high cholesterol because they help lower the levels of bad cholesterol (LDL) in the blood by increasing the excretion of bile acids and improving the function of the liver, which in turn reduces the risk of heart disease.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia miltiorrhiza is a type of plant that has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for a long time. It's known to help lower high cholesterol levels in the blood. This is because it has a compound called tanshinone, which helps to relax the blood vessels and improve blood flow. When blood vessels are relaxed, it's easier for the blood to flow, which reduces the pressure on the heart. This, in turn, helps to lower the levels of bad cholesterol, or LDL, in the blood. High levels of LDL can cause plaque buildup in the arteries, leading to heart problems. Salvia miltiorrhiza decoctions can also help to increase the levels of good cholesterol, or HDL, in the blood. HDL helps to remove excess cholesterol from the blood and bring it back to the liver, where it can be removed from the body. By lowering bad cholesterol and increasing good cholesterol, Salvia miltiorrhiza decoctions can help to keep the heart healthy and reduce the risk of heart problems.  Salvia miltiorrhiza also contains compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol. When the body is inflamed, it can release chemicals that make the blood vessels more likely to constrict, which can raise blood pressure and contribute to high cholesterol. By reducing inflammation, Salvia miltiorrhiza decoctions can help to keep the blood vessels relaxed and the blood flowing freely. Additionally, Salvia miltiorrhiza has been shown to have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the blood vessels from damage caused by free radicals. Free radicals are unstable molecules that can damage the blood vessels and contribute to high cholesterol. By protecting the blood vessels from damage, Salvia miltiorrhiza decoctions can help to keep the heart healthy and reduce the risk of heart problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia miltiorrhiza herbal decoction that helps with high cholesterol, first you need to gather the necessary herbs. Choose high-quality Salvia miltiorrhiza roots, which are the part of the plant used for medicinal purposes. Rinse the roots with clean water to remove any dirt or debris. Next, chop the roots into small pieces to increase their surface area and help them release their active compounds more easily. In a medium-sized pot, combine the chopped roots with 4-6 cups of water and bring the mixture to a boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to a simmer and let it cook for 30-40 minutes, allowing the roots to release their medicinal properties into the water. After the decoction has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container, discarding the solids. The resulting liquid is your Salvia miltiorrhiza decoction, which can be consumed 2-3 times a day as needed to help lower cholesterol levels and support cardiovascular health.

intro_desc: High cholesterol can be a serious health issue, increasing the risk of heart disease and stroke. Fixing it is crucial because it can help prevent these conditions and improve your overall well-being. One way to manage high cholesterol is through herbal decoctions, which can be made from various herbs that have been shown to have a positive effect on cholesterol levels. Ginkgo biloba, for example, has been found to help lower LDL (bad) cholesterol and increase HDL (good) cholesterol. This is because it improves blood flow and reduces inflammation in the blood vessels. Allium sativum, also known as garlic, has been shown to have a similar effect, with its active compounds helping to reduce LDL cholesterol and improve overall cardiovascular health.  Herbal decoctions can be a good alternative to medication because they are often safer and have fewer side effects. Additionally, they can be made at home and are relatively inexpensive. However, it's essential to note that while herbal decoctions can be beneficial, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. If you have high cholesterol, it's best to consult with your doctor before starting any new supplements or treatments.
supplementary_best: A combination of Ginkgo biloba, Salvia miltiorrhiza, and Cinnamomum verum can be effective in lowering high cholesterol. Ginkgo biloba helps by improving blood circulation and reducing inflammation in the blood vessels, making it easier for blood to flow and reducing the risk of clot formation. Salvia miltiorrhiza, also known as "red sage" or "tang kuei," has been traditionally used to treat cardiovascular conditions, as it increases blood flow and improves the function of blood vessels. Cinnamomum verum, or "true cinnamon," has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the oxidation of low-density lipoprotein (LDL) cholesterol and prevent it from building up in the arteries.
supplementary_avoid: If you have high cholesterol, it's best to avoid herbal decoctions that can raise cholesterol levels or worsen heart health. One such herb is 'Trifolium pratense' or Red Clover, which is high in isoflavones that can lower 'good' cholesterol levels (HDL) while raising 'bad' cholesterol levels (LDL).  Another herb to avoid is 'Catha edulis' or Khat, which can increase heart rate and blood pressure, making it a risk for those with high cholesterol.  Additionally, avoid 'Silybum marianum' or Milk Thistle, which can interact with medications used to treat high cholesterol, such as statins, and reduce their effectiveness.  Lastly, 'Vitex agnus-castus' or Chasteberry may also be a concern for those with high cholesterol. It may increase blood pressure and worsen heart conditions due to its stimulant properties.  These herbs should be used with caution, and it's always best to consult with a healthcare professional before consuming any herbal decoctions, especially if you have pre-existing health conditions or take medications.
supplementary_other_remedies: Eating foods rich in fiber like fruits, vegetables, and whole grains can help lower cholesterol levels. This is because fiber helps remove excess cholesterol from the body and prevents it from being absorbed into the bloodstream. Foods high in soluble fiber, such as oats, barley, and fruits like apples and berries, are particularly effective at reducing cholesterol levels. Additionally, incorporating healthy fats like omega-3 fatty acids found in fatty fish, nuts, and seeds into the diet can also help lower cholesterol. This is because these healthy fats help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol. Exercise, such as walking or other aerobic activities, can also help lower cholesterol levels by improving circulation and reducing inflammation. Furthermore, getting enough probiotics, found in fermented foods like yogurt and kefir, can help maintain a healthy gut microbiome, which is essential for proper cholesterol regulation.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with high cholesterol often experience other health problems. One common issue is heart disease, which can lead to chest pain, heart attacks, and strokes. This is because high cholesterol can clog arteries, making it harder for blood to flow. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels and arteries.  Another problem is high blood pressure, which can also cause heart disease. Herbs like hawthorn and passionflower can help lower blood pressure by relaxing the blood vessels and improving circulation. These herbs also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals.  High cholesterol can also lead to kidney disease, which can cause fatigue, swelling, and difficulty urinating. Medicinal herbs like dandelion root and juniper berries can help support kidney function by improving urine production and reducing fluid buildup.  People with high cholesterol may also experience digestive problems, such as constipation and bloating. Herbs like psyllium and aloe vera can help regulate bowel movements and soothe the digestive system.  Additionally, high cholesterol can increase the risk of blood clots, which can lead to deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism. Herbs like ginkgo biloba and cayenne pepper can help improve blood circulation and reduce the risk of blood clots.  Finally, people with high cholesterol may experience cognitive decline and memory loss, which can be caused by inflammation and oxidative stress in the brain. Herbs like ginkgo biloba and ashwagandha can help reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function.  It's essential to note that while medicinal herbs can help alleviate these symptoms, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. Consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedies, especially if you're already taking medication or have underlying health conditions.
